title: Acne Treatment
tagline: Clear Skin Changes Everything — Medical Acne Treatment in Durban
quickSummary: Acne is a medical condition — not a hygiene problem. **Dr. Bangalee** provides comprehensive, clinically proven **acne treatment** programmes that address the root cause of your specific acne: in-clinic treatments combined with a medically tailored home-care regimen that delivers lasting results.
priceFrom: R 850 – R 2,500 per session (package pricing available)
downtime: Minimal — 24–48 hours mild redness or peeling post-peel treatment
downtimeDetail: **Chemical peel** treatments: mild peeling, redness, and sensitivity for 2–5 days. **Prescription skincare**: some initial dryness or irritation as skin adapts. **Dermapen** (for acne scarring): 24–48 hours redness, 3–5 days minor social downtime.
suitableFor: **Teenagers and young adults** with persistent or severe breakouts that aren't responding to over-the-counter products.
**Adult women with hormonal acne** — breakouts along the jaw, chin, and neck that worsen around menstruation, with the pill, or during perimenopause.
Anyone whose acne is **affecting confidence** at work, in social situations, or in front of a camera.
Those dealing with **post-acne scarring, dark marks** (post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation), or pitting.
People ready to stop cycling through products and finally get a **medical plan that actually works**.
heroText: You've tried **every cream and cleanser on the shelf**. You've adjusted your diet, followed the skincare routines, and read every article. And yet — every morning brings the same quiet disappointment, the same reaching for concealer, the same wishing it would just stop. **Acne is not a hygiene problem.** It's not something you caused, and it's not something you should have to figure out alone. It is a **medical condition with a medical solution** — and at Star Aesthetic Centre, **Dr. Bangalee treats it exactly that way**: clinically, comprehensively, and with a plan built around the root cause of your specific acne.
whatIs:
There is something quietly exhausting about **managing acne as an adult**. The morning mirror check. The decisions about which events to attend, which photos to allow. The way a single breakout can knock your confidence on a day that matters — a job interview, a client presentation, a first date. **You are not overreacting.** Research confirms what you already know: acne has measurable, documented impacts on self-esteem, professional confidence, and social engagement.

Medically, acne is a **chronic inflammatory condition of the pilosebaceous unit** — the hair follicle and its associated oil gland. When excess sebum combines with dead skin cell build-up, it creates the perfect environment for Cutibacterium acnes (the acne-causing bacterium) to proliferate, triggering the inflammation that produces pimples, papules, pustules, nodules, and cysts.

At Star Aesthetic, **Dr. Bangalee treats every type of acne** — from mild comedonal acne (blackheads and whiteheads) and inflammatory acne to severe nodulo-cystic and hormonal acne in adult women. Treatment is always clinical, always personalised, and always designed to address the **underlying cause** — not just the surface symptom.

**Acne is not caused by poor hygiene, eating chocolate, or laziness.** It is a medical condition with genetic, hormonal, and environmental drivers — and it deserves proper medical attention.
howWorks: Comprehensive Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee examines your skin, reviews your full history (diet, stress, hormonal factors, previous treatments), and **classifies your exact acne type and grade** to build an accurate clinical picture.
Personalised Treatment Plan — A **customised protocol is designed** combining in-clinic treatments with a medically tailored home-care regimen — because no two skins are the same.

In-Clinic Treatment — Depending on acne severity: **chemical peels** (salicylic acid, glycolic acid, or mesoestetic mesopeel), **Dermapen microneedling** for acne scarring, or mesoestetic acnelan treatment.

Home-Care Programme — **Pharmaceutical-grade skincare prescribed** from our professional range: blemiderm® resurfacing gel, mesoprotech® SPF, mesoestetic Silymarin CF, or NeoStrata Clarify — matched to your skin type.
Prescription Medication if Required — Topical or oral prescription treatments recommended for **moderate-to-severe or antibiotic-resistant** cases when clinically indicated.
Follow-Up & Adjustment — Regular review appointments **monitor progress and fine-tune the protocol** — because effective acne management is a programme, not a single appointment.
expectedResults: **Weeks 1–2:** Some patients experience a brief purging phase as clogged pores clear — this is expected and a sign the treatment is working. **Weeks 3–4:** Reduction in new breakouts; improved skin texture and tone. **Weeks 6–8:** Significantly clearer skin; existing dark marks beginning to fade noticeably. **Weeks 10–12:** Substantial improvement in skin clarity — with the right home-care, results continue to build beyond 12 weeks.

faqs.question: What is the best acne treatment in Durban?
faqs.answer: There is no single 'best' — **effective acne management depends on your specific acne type, skin type, severity, and contributing factors**. At Star Aesthetic, Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough assessment and creates a personalised plan that typically combines in-clinic treatments (chemical peels, microneedling) with pharmaceutical-grade home-care skincare. Book a consultation to receive your personalised recommendation.

faqs.question: Can adults get acne treatment — or is this just for teenagers?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — **adult acne is extremely common**, particularly in women, and is one of the most frequent concerns we see. Hormonal acne in adult women — which often presents along the jaw, chin, and neck — requires specific treatment approaches different from teenage acne. Dr. Bangalee regularly treats patients in their 30s, 40s, and 50s.

faqs.question: How many treatments will I need?
faqs.answer: Most patients see **meaningful improvement within 3–6 in-clinic sessions**, combined with consistent daily home-care skincare. More severe or cystic acne may require a longer programme. Dr. Bangalee will give you a clear, honest expectation at your first consultation.

faqs.question: Will my acne come back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Acne has a tendency to recur if underlying triggers are not addressed. However, **with the right skincare programme and periodic maintenance visits, most patients maintain clear skin long-term**. We will equip you with the knowledge and tools to manage any future flares quickly.

faqs.question: Is the treatment painful?
faqs.answer: Chemical peels cause **mild tingling or warmth** — most patients tolerate them very well. Dermapen microneedling uses topical anaesthetic and is described as a mild vibration sensation. In-clinic acne treatments are designed to be comfortable.

faqs.question: What causes adult hormonal acne?
faqs.answer: Hormonal acne in adult women is driven by **fluctuations in androgens** that stimulate excess sebum production. Common triggers include menstrual cycle fluctuations, perimenopause, stopping or starting hormonal contraception, **polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS)**, and stress (which elevates cortisol, which in turn raises androgen levels).

faqs.question: What is the difference between acne and rosacea?
faqs.answer: Acne and rosacea can look similar but are distinct conditions. **Acne involves clogged pores, comedones, and bacterial-driven inflammation.** Rosacea is a vascular condition with redness, visible blood vessels, and papules but no comedones. A professional assessment is essential to distinguish between the two — treatment approaches are completely different.

faqs.question: Can you treat acne scarring as well?
faqs.answer: Yes. **Acne scarring** — including icepick scars, boxcar scars, rolling scars, and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (dark marks) — can be significantly improved through **Dermapen microneedling, chemical peels, and mesoestetic professional treatments**. We treat both active acne and its aftermath.

faqs.question: What skincare products should I use for acne-prone skin?
faqs.answer: The right products depend on your skin type and acne severity. We recommend **pharmaceutical-grade cosmeceuticals** from our shop range: mesoestetic blemiderm® Resurfacing Gel (evening treatment), SkinCeuticals Silymarin CF (morning antioxidant for oily skin), and mesoprotech® mineral fluid SPF50+ (daily sun protection). A complete protocol is prescribed at consultation.

faqs.question: Will acne treatment help with the dark marks my spots leave behind?
faqs.answer: Yes — **post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation** (the dark marks left after spots heal) is addressed as part of our acne management programme. Chemical peels and the melan tran3x home-care range are particularly effective for these marks.

faqs.question: What should I avoid eating to help my acne?
faqs.answer: The strongest evidence is for **high-glycaemic index foods** (white bread, sugary drinks, white rice) and dairy products in some individuals — these spike insulin and IGF-1, stimulating sebum production. However, **diet alone is rarely sufficient to clear moderate or severe acne** — medical treatment is the primary approach, with diet as a supporting factor.

title: Body Contouring
tagline: Non-Surgical Fat Reduction & Body Sculpting in Durban
quickSummary: Body contouring uses advanced **non-invasive technology** to selectively reduce **localised fat deposits** and tighten skin — without surgery, without general anaesthesia, and without extended recovery periods.
priceFrom: R 1,200 – R 3,500 per session (area and modality dependent)
downtime: Minimal — return to normal activities immediately
downtimeDetail: **Zero clinical downtime** — this is one of the primary advantages of non-surgical body contouring over surgical liposuction. Mild redness, warmth, or temporary numbness in the treated area may occur immediately post-treatment and resolves within hours.
suitableFor: Adults at or near their **healthy body weight** with specific, localised fat deposits.
Those who have achieved **diet and exercise goals** but still have stubborn 'problem areas'.
Men and women wanting **body sculpting without surgical downtime**.
Patients preparing for or recovering from major life events — **weddings, milestone birthdays**.
Those seeking a **non-surgical alternative to liposuction**.
heroText: Frustrated with **stubborn fat** that refuses to respond to diet and exercise? Body contouring at Star Aesthetic Centre offers **non-surgical, clinically proven fat reduction** and body sculpting treatments — targeting those problem areas that lifestyle changes alone cannot reach.
whatIs:
**Body contouring** is the collective term for non-surgical aesthetic procedures designed to reduce localised fat deposits, tighten skin, and sculpt specific areas of the body. At Star Aesthetic, our body contouring treatments target the stubborn fat pockets that are genetically resistant to diet and exercise — the **love handles, lower abdominal pouch, inner thighs, upper arms, and submental (under-chin) fat**.

Non-surgical body contouring is not a **weight loss treatment** — it is a precision body sculpting procedure for patients who are at or near their ideal weight but have localised fat areas they wish to reduce. It is the medical alternative to liposuction for patients who want meaningful fat reduction without surgery.
howWorks: Consultation & Body Analysis — Dr. Bangalee assesses your specific areas of concern, skin quality, and overall health to determine the most appropriate **contouring modality**.
Treatment Mapping — Target areas are precisely mapped and photographed to **track progress**.
In-Clinic Treatment — Advanced body contouring technology is applied to target areas. The treatment **selectively acts on fat cells** while leaving surrounding tissue unaffected.
Fat Cell Elimination — Treated fat cells undergo a **natural metabolic elimination process**, gradually reducing the fat layer over 4–12 weeks.
Progressive Sessions — Multiple sessions are typically required for optimal results, spaced **2–4 weeks apart**.
Results Assessment — Progress photographs at each visit track **measurable improvements** in circumference and body shape.
expectedResults: **Weeks 2–4:** Subtle early changes may be visible. **Weeks 6–8:** Noticeable reduction in the treated area. **Weeks 10–12:** Optimal results visible as treated fat cells are fully eliminated. Ongoing improvement may occur for up to **6 months** post final session.

faqs.question: What is the difference between body contouring and weight loss?
faqs.answer: Body contouring reduces **localised fat deposits** in specific areas — it does not produce overall weight loss. It is most effective for patients at or near their goal weight who have stubborn areas that don't respond to diet and exercise. If you have significant weight to lose, we recommend achieving your weight loss goals first, then using body contouring to refine the result.

faqs.question: Is non-surgical body contouring as effective as liposuction?
faqs.answer: For the right candidate, non-surgical body contouring delivers excellent, measurable results without any surgical risk, anaesthesia, or extended recovery. **Liposuction** typically delivers more dramatic single-treatment results but comes with surgical risks and recovery time. Dr. Bangalee will advise which approach best suits your goals and starting point.

faqs.question: Does body contouring hurt?
faqs.answer: No — non-surgical body contouring treatments are designed to be comfortable. Most patients experience a **warming sensation or mild suction pressure**. No anaesthesia or recovery is required.

faqs.question: How many sessions do I need?
faqs.answer: Most patients require **4–8 sessions** for optimal results, depending on the area and the degree of fat reduction desired. Dr. Bangalee will give you a clear treatment plan with expected number of sessions at your initial consultation.

faqs.question: Will the fat come back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Fat cells that are eliminated by body contouring **do not regenerate** — those cells are permanently gone. However, remaining fat cells in the body can enlarge with weight gain. Maintaining a healthy lifestyle protects and maximises your results.

faqs.question: Can body contouring help with loose skin?
faqs.answer: Depending on the degree of skin laxity and the technology used, some body contouring treatments also provide a **skin tightening effect**. Dr. Bangalee will assess your skin elasticity at consultation and recommend the most appropriate approach.

faqs.question: Who is NOT a good candidate for body contouring?
faqs.answer: Body contouring is not appropriate for **pregnant or breastfeeding women**, those with certain medical conditions (including active infections or implanted devices in the treatment area), or those with unrealistic expectations about outcomes. A thorough medical assessment at consultation determines candidacy.

faqs.question: Can I use body contouring to lose belly fat?
faqs.answer: Yes — the **abdomen** is one of the most commonly treated areas. Non-surgical body contouring can meaningfully reduce the lower abdominal pouch and flanks (love handles). Results vary by individual, and multiple sessions are typically needed for significant abdominal fat reduction.

faqs.question: How long before I see results?
faqs.answer: Early subtle changes appear from **4–6 weeks** after your first session. Optimal results are typically visible at 10–12 weeks after your final session, as the body's natural elimination process completes.

faqs.question: Is body contouring safe?
faqs.answer: Non-surgical body contouring technologies used at Star Aesthetic are **clinically validated** and have extensive safety records. Dr. Bangalee conducts a full medical assessment before any treatment to ensure your safety and suitability.

title: Anti-Wrinkle Treatment
tagline: Soften lines. Look refreshed. Feel like yourself again.
quickSummary: The **Anti-Wrinkle Treatment** at Star Aesthetic Centre uses **Botulinum Toxin** (Botox®) — a purified protein that temporarily relaxes the facial muscles responsible for **expression lines**, smoothing frown lines, forehead creases, and crow's feet with zero downtime.
priceFrom: From R 90 per unit · Optimal dosing (64 units) R 5,440

notes: Unit requirements vary per patient and are assessed at consultation. A consultation fee applies for treatments under 15 units.

downtime: Zero downtime — return to normal activities immediately
downtimeDetail: **Zero clinical downtime.** You can return to work, driving, and most normal activities immediately. Avoid strenuous exercise, alcohol, facial massage, and lying face-down for **24 hours** post-treatment.
suitableFor: Women and men with forehead lines, frown lines, or crow's feet they wish to soften.
Those wanting a **preventive approach** — starting treatment before expression lines become deeply etched.
Patients who want a **non-surgical, zero-downtime** solution for facial rejuvenation.
Those with **excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis)** in underarms, hands, or feet.
Those looking to prolong and extend their Anti-Wrinkle Treatment results with **SkinCeuticals P-TIOX** at home.
heroText: Fine lines and expression wrinkles don't have to define how you look or feel. Our **Anti-Wrinkle Treatment** is performed using **Botox®** (Botulinum Toxin), administered exclusively by **Dr. Rajeev Bangalee** — an experienced aesthetic physician whose philosophy is **natural-looking results**. Look refreshed, rested, and like the best version of yourself.
whatIs:
Anti-wrinkle treatments such as Botox® and Xeomin® (Botulinum Toxin Type A) are purified neurotoxin proteins that temporarily block nerve signals to specific facial muscles, causing them to relax. When the muscles responsible for creating expression lines — such as forehead creases, frown lines, and crow's feet — are temporarily relaxed, the overlying skin appears smoother, softer, and more refreshed.

Anti-wrinkle treatments do not fill lines — they relax the muscles that create them. This makes them ideal for treating dynamic wrinkles (lines caused by facial movement).

At Star Aesthetic Centre, every anti-wrinkle treatment is administered personally by Dr. Bangalee using precise anatomical knowledge and years of aesthetic experience. His philosophy is always centred around natural-looking results — subtle enhancement that leaves you looking refreshed, never overdone.
howWorks: Consultation — Dr. Bangalee discusses your concerns, assesses your **facial anatomy**, and creates a precise treatment plan. For first-time patients, photographs are taken for comparison.
Preparation — The treatment area is cleansed. **Topical anaesthetic cream** is optional for sensitive patients but rarely required for this treatment.
Injection — Using a very fine needle, precise amounts of **Botulinum Toxin** are injected at specific anatomical points. The injections take 5–15 minutes.
Post-Treatment Care — You will receive written aftercare instructions. Sit upright for **4 hours**; avoid strenuous exercise, saunas, and lying face-down for 24 hours.
Onset — Results begin appearing within **3–5 days** as the toxin takes effect.
2-Week Review — A complimentary review appointment at **2 weeks** allows Dr. Bangalee to assess the result and make any fine-tuning adjustments.
expectedResults: **Day 3–5:** Muscles begin to relax and initial softening of lines is visible. **Day 10–14:** Full effect is visible — this is the standard point at which results are evaluated. **Duration: 3–5 months**, depending on metabolism, muscle activity, and units used. With consistent treatment every 3–4 months, some patients find they need less product over time as muscles weaken with regular relaxation.

faqs.question: How long does the Anti-Wrinkle Treatment last?
faqs.answer: Results typically last **3–5 months**. First-time patients often find it lasts closer to 3 months; with regular repeat treatments every 3–4 months, duration can extend to 5 months or longer as the targeted muscles weaken and require less product to maintain relaxation.

faqs.question: Will the Anti-Wrinkle Treatment make me look frozen?
faqs.answer: Not when performed by an experienced aesthetic physician. **Dr. Bangalee's philosophy is always natural** — conservative dosing and precise anatomical placement soften lines while preserving natural facial expression. A 'frozen' result is caused by excessive dosing and poor technique, not botulinum toxin itself.

faqs.question: Does the Anti-Wrinkle Treatment hurt?
faqs.answer: The needle used is extremely fine (**30–32 gauge** — similar to an insulin needle) and injections are very brief. Most patients describe a mild sting lasting 1–2 seconds. The treatment is well-tolerated without anaesthetic for most patients.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Anti-Wrinkle Treatment and dermal fillers?
faqs.answer: **Anti-Wrinkle Treatment** (using botulinum toxin) relaxes the muscles that create dynamic expression lines — forehead lines, frown lines, and crow's feet. **Dermal fillers** restore volume, lift, and contour — addressing static lines, volume loss, and lip and cheek enhancement. The two treatments are frequently combined for comprehensive facial rejuvenation.

faqs.question: At what age should I start Anti-Wrinkle Treatment?
faqs.answer: There is no single 'right age.' Some patients start in their **late 20s** as a preventive measure — using Anti-Wrinkle Treatment before lines become deeply etched. Most patients begin in their 30s or 40s when expression lines start to bother them at rest. Dr. Bangalee will advise the right timing based on your individual anatomy and concerns.

faqs.question: Is botulinum toxin (Botox®) safe?
faqs.answer: Yes. **Botulinum Toxin** has one of the most extensive safety records in medicine — it has been used clinically for over 30 years and is approved by both the **FDA and SAHPRA**. Administered by a qualified and experienced aesthetic physician like Dr. Bangalee, serious complications are exceptionally rare.

faqs.question: Can Anti-Wrinkle Treatment stop excessive sweating?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the most effective applications of botulinum toxin. Injected into the **underarms, hands, or feet**, it blocks the nerve signals to sweat glands, dramatically reducing sweating for **6–9 months**. Many patients consider this their most impactful treatment.

faqs.question: Can I exercise after my Anti-Wrinkle Treatment?
faqs.answer: Avoid strenuous exercise for **24 hours** after treatment. Exercise increases blood flow and can cause the toxin to migrate from the injection site before it has fully localised, potentially affecting unintended muscles.

faqs.question: Can men have Anti-Wrinkle Treatment?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — and Anti-Wrinkle Treatment for men is increasingly popular. Men typically require higher doses due to stronger facial muscles and may need slightly different injection points to preserve a naturally masculine appearance. Dr. Bangalee treats male patients regularly.

faqs.question: What is the SkinCeuticals P-TIOX serum — can it replace botulinum toxin injections?
faqs.answer: **P-TIOX** is a peptide serum inspired by the science of botulinum toxin — it reduces the appearance of expression lines topically. It is not as immediately effective as injectable Anti-Wrinkle Treatment, but it delivers real results and is excellent for patients who want to extend their treatment results or prefer a non-injectable approach. Dr. Bangalee can advise which is right for your goals.

faqs.question: How do I find a qualified practitioner for Anti-Wrinkle Treatment in Durban?
faqs.answer: Always seek Anti-Wrinkle Treatment from a **qualified, registered medical practitioner** — not a beauty salon or untrained injector. **Dr. Rajeev Bangalee** is a qualified medical doctor registered with the **HPCSA** (Health Professions Council of South Africa) with specific aesthetic medicine training. Book at Star Aesthetic Centre on **+27 (0)31 573 1325**.

title: Dermapen® Microneedling Treatment
tagline: Clinically Proven Skin Rejuvenation — Stimulating Your Skin's Own Collagen
quickSummary: **Dermapen microneedling** creates thousands of micro-channels in the skin, triggering a controlled healing response that stimulates **collagen and elastin production**. The result: visibly improved texture, reduced scarring, tighter pores, and a younger-looking complexion.
downtimeDetail: **Social downtime** of 24–48 hours due to redness. Makeup can typically be applied from day 3 post-treatment. Avoid direct sun exposure, swimming, and strenuous exercise for **48–72 hours**. No saunas for 7 days.
suitableFor: Women and men with **acne scarring** — pitting, icepick scars, rolling scars, or boxcar scars.
Those with **enlarged pores, rough skin texture**, or generally dull, tired-looking skin.
Patients with **fine lines and early signs of skin ageing** wanting a non-injectable rejuvenation.
Those with **stretch marks** (body) or skin laxity post-weight loss.
Patients with **hair loss** (scalp microneedling with growth factors).
Anyone wanting to maximise the absorption of their **home-care skincare products** (microneedling dramatically improves product penetration).
heroText: **Dermapen microneedling** is one of the most versatile and effective skin rejuvenation treatments available — addressing everything from fine lines and acne scarring to enlarged pores and skin texture. At Star Aesthetic, **Dr. Bangalee** uses the authentic **Dermapen®** — the world's leading medical-grade microneedling device.
whatIs:
**Dermapen® microneedling** is a minimally invasive skin rejuvenation procedure that uses a medical-grade device with multiple ultra-fine needles to create thousands of microscopic punctures in the skin's surface. These controlled micro-injuries trigger the skin's natural wound-healing response — stimulating the production of new **collagen and elastin fibres** that replace damaged or aged skin tissue.

Unlike ablative laser treatments that remove the outer skin layer, microneedling stimulates regeneration from within while keeping the surface mostly intact. This means more effective healing, less risk of **pigmentation changes** (important for darker South African skin tones), and a significantly faster recovery than ablative alternatives.

**Dermapen®** is the authentic, globally recognised gold standard in microneedling devices — used by aesthetic physicians, dermatologists, and plastic surgeons worldwide. At Star Aesthetic, every treatment is performed by **Dr. Bangalee** personally.
howWorks: Consultation & Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your skin concerns, type, and treatment history to design your **Dermapen protocol** and select appropriate serums for infusion during treatment.
Topical Anaesthetic Application — **EMLA cream** is applied and left for 30–45 minutes to ensure your comfort.
Cleansing & Preparation — Skin is thoroughly cleansed and prepared. A sterile, **single-use cartridge** is loaded into the Dermapen®.

Microneedling Treatment — The Dermapen® is passed over the skin in methodical passes, creating controlled micro-channels. **Therapeutic serums** (hyaluronic acid, growth factors, vitamin C) are applied simultaneously for deep infusion.
Post-Treatment Application — **Soothing serum and SPF** are applied. Aftercare instructions are provided in writing.
Recovery & Results — Skin appears red (similar to a mild sunburn) for **24–48 hours**, then settles. New collagen formation continues over 4–6 weeks.
expectedResults: **Days 1–3:** Skin appears pink/red and may feel tight — this is the active healing phase. **Week 1–2:** Redness resolves; skin may feel smoother early. **Weeks 3–4:** New collagen formation begins to improve texture and pore size. **Weeks 4–8:** Significant improvement in skin quality, scarring, and overall radiance visible. **Weeks 8–12:** Full results of the treatment cycle are visible — typically requiring **3–6 sessions** total.

faqs.question: Is Dermapen microneedling safe for darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the key advantages of Dermapen over ablative laser for South African patients. Microneedling does not use heat or light energy, meaning it does not carry the **pigmentation risk** (post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation or hypopigmentation) that some laser treatments can cause in **Fitzpatrick IV–VI skin tones**. It is safe and effective for all skin tones.

faqs.question: How many Dermapen sessions do I need?
faqs.answer: For general skin rejuvenation and anti-ageing, **3 sessions** (4–6 weeks apart) deliver excellent results. For **acne scarring**, 4–6 sessions are typically needed for significant improvement. Severe scarring may require more. Dr. Bangalee will give you a realistic expectation at your first consultation.

faqs.question: Does microneedling hurt?
faqs.answer: **Topical anaesthetic cream** is applied 30–45 minutes before treatment, making Dermapen very well-tolerated. Most patients describe a mild vibrating or scratching sensation rather than pain. The face may feel flushed and warm immediately after — this settles within hours.

faqs.question: How long does Dermapen last?
faqs.answer: The new collagen stimulated by Dermapen continues to remodel for up to **6 months** post-treatment. Results from a full series of 3–6 treatments can last **12–18 months**, depending on skin type, age, and lifestyle. Quarterly maintenance treatments are recommended for ongoing rejuvenation.

faqs.question: Can Dermapen help with acne scars?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of Dermapen's most impactful applications. Microneedling breaks down the **fibrous scar tissue** that creates textural irregularities and stimulates the formation of new, smoother collagen. Multiple sessions are required for significant scar improvement — typically **4–6 sessions** for moderate to severe acne scarring.

faqs.question: What serums are used during Dermapen treatment?
faqs.answer: Dr. Bangalee selects serums based on your specific concern: **hyaluronic acid** for hydration and plumping, **vitamin C** for brightening and antioxidant support, **growth factor serums** for enhanced healing and collagen stimulation, or **platelet-rich plasma (PRP)** for advanced skin regeneration. The micro-channels created by Dermapen dramatically increase serum absorption.

faqs.question: When can I wear makeup after microneedling?
faqs.answer: Avoid makeup for the first **24–48 hours** to allow the micro-channels to close and prevent the risk of infection. From day 3, mineral powder foundation is appropriate. Full makeup can typically resume from **day 3–5** depending on how quickly your skin heals.

faqs.question: Can I get Dermapen done if I have active acne?
faqs.answer: Active inflamed acne is a **contraindication** for microneedling over the affected areas — the risk of spreading bacteria and worsening infection is significant. We recommend managing active acne first, then using Dermapen to address the residual scarring. Dr. Bangalee will assess and advise appropriately.

faqs.question: Is Dermapen better than a home derma-roller?
faqs.answer: Dramatically so. Dermapen uses **medical-grade, sterile, single-use cartridges** with precisely controlled needle depth (0.1mm–2.5mm). Home rollers use dull, fixed-depth needles that create tearing rather than clean micro-channels, carry significant infection risk, and cannot achieve the depths required for collagen stimulation. Never use a home roller on **active acne scarring** — it can worsen scarring.

faqs.question: Can Dermapen tighten loose skin?
faqs.answer: Yes. The **collagen stimulation** from microneedling improves skin elasticity and can provide a meaningful tightening effect — particularly in areas of mild laxity like the lower face, neck, and jowls. For more significant skin laxity, Dr. Bangalee may recommend combining Dermapen with other treatments.

title: Excessive Sweating Treatment (Hyperhidrosis)
tagline: Reclaim Your Confidence — Medical Hyperhidrosis Treatment in Durban
quickSummary: **Excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis)** is a medical condition with a highly effective medical solution. A single **30-minute treatment** with Botulinum Toxin (Botox®) delivers **6–9 months** of dramatic relief — no surgery, no downtime, and a result that quietly transforms how you show up in every area of your life.

notes: Hyperhidrosis typically requires 80+ units per treatment area. At R 85/unit, underarm treatment starts from approximately R 6,800. Exact dosage assessed at consultation.

downtimeDetail: **Zero.** You can return to work, dress normally, and resume all activities immediately. Avoid strenuous exercise and hot baths for 24 hours.
suitableFor: Anyone whose sweating is **excessive, unpredictable, or quietly limiting** their professional or social life.
People who **plan their wardrobe, seating, and daily schedule** around avoiding visible sweat.
Those who have tried prescription-strength antiperspirants and found them **inadequate or short-lived**.
Professionals who **avoid handshakes, presentations, or client meetings** because of sweating.
Those with **sweaty palms** that affect professional interactions, writing, or using devices.
Anyone who has been **managing this alone for years** and is ready to stop managing and start living freely.
heroText: You check your shirt before every meeting. You choose dark colours every morning — **not by style preference, but by necessity**. You've declined handshakes. Avoided certain seats. **Quietly planned your life around your sweating.** This is not weakness. This is not anxiety you just need to manage better. This is **hyperhidrosis — a medical condition** that affects millions of people and has a highly effective, long-lasting medical solution. At Star Aesthetic Centre, **a single 30-minute treatment** delivers 6 to 9 months of complete relief — changing how you show up at work, in relationships, and in every room you walk into.
whatIs:
**Excessive sweating is one of the most under-treated medical conditions** — partly because people feel embarrassed to raise it, and partly because most don't know that a real, lasting medical solution exists.

If your sweating is unpredictable, occurs regardless of temperature, and has **quietly begun to shrink your world** — the events you attend, the clothes you wear, the professional opportunities you pursue — you meet the clinical criteria for **primary focal hyperhidrosis**: a condition where the nerve signals to the sweat glands are chronically overactive, far beyond what normal temperature regulation requires.

The **professional impact is significant** and rarely spoken about openly. Research shows people with hyperhidrosis report lower confidence in job interviews, reduced willingness to take on client-facing roles, and real impact on career decisions. **You already know this.** You've felt it in every handshake you've hesitated over, every presentation you've dreaded, every opportunity that felt smaller because of how you felt in your own body.

At Star Aesthetic, we treat hyperhidrosis using **Botulinum Toxin (Botox®)** — the most evidence-based, effective medical treatment for this condition. Injected into the underarms, palms, or soles, the toxin blocks the nerve signal to the sweat glands, **dramatically reducing sweat production for 6–9 months from a single 30-minute session**. One treatment. Six to nine months of freedom.
howWorks: Consultation & Clinical Assessment — Dr. Bangalee reviews your sweating history, **confirms the hyperhidrosis diagnosis**, and discusses which areas to treat in one comprehensive appointment.
Starch-Iodine Mapping — A simple, painless test **precisely maps the areas of heaviest sweating activity** — the injection grid is drawn to treat exactly where it's needed, not broadly.
Topical Anaesthetic (Optional) — Anaesthetic cream is available for the underarm area if preferred. For palms and feet, a **nerve block is offered** for maximum comfort.
Injections — Multiple small, precisely placed injections of Botulinum Toxin across the mapped zone using a **very fine needle**. The procedure takes 15–20 minutes.
Immediate Return to Life — You dress normally and **return to work, meetings, and life immediately** after the procedure. No recovery. No visible signs of treatment.
expectedResults: **Days 5–7:** Initial reduction in sweating becomes noticeable. **Day 14:** Full effect — sweating in treated areas is **dramatically reduced or eliminated**. **Duration: 6–9 months** — significantly longer than cosmetic Botox, and consistent with each repeat treatment. Most patients describe the result as genuinely life-changing for their **professional and social confidence**.

faqs.question: How do I know if my sweating is medically excessive?
faqs.answer: If your sweating **occurs regardless of temperature or exercise, is unpredictable, affects both sides equally**, and interferes with your daily life or emotional wellbeing — these are the diagnostic criteria for **primary hyperhidrosis**. Dr. Bangalee will conduct a clinical assessment to confirm the diagnosis and recommend the right treatment approach.

faqs.question: How long does Botox for sweating last?
faqs.answer: For hyperhidrosis, Botox typically lasts **6–9 months** — significantly longer than the 3–5 months for cosmetic facial Botox. This is because the sweat glands require higher doses for sustained effect, and there is no dynamic muscle activity to break down the toxin faster.

faqs.question: Will Botox for sweating affect my health — do I need to sweat to regulate temperature?
faqs.answer: No. The body has **2–4 million sweat glands** — treating a small area of underarms, hands, or feet blocks only a tiny fraction of total sweat capacity. Your body's overall **thermoregulatory capacity** is completely unaffected. You will still sweat normally everywhere else.

faqs.question: Is the treatment painful?
faqs.answer: Underarm injections are **well-tolerated**, with mild stinging at each injection point. Hands and feet are more sensitive areas — we offer a **nerve block** for palmar treatment. Most patients say the procedure is **much more comfortable than anticipated**.

faqs.question: Can excessive sweating come back after Botox?
faqs.answer: Yes — the treatment is temporary, lasting **6–9 months**. As Botox gradually wears off, sweating returns to its previous level. **Repeat treatment restores the same degree of relief**. There is no evidence that Botox becomes less effective with repeated treatments over time.

faqs.question: Will I have compensatory sweating in other areas after treatment?
faqs.answer: A small percentage of patients experience a mild increase in sweating in adjacent areas — this is more common with very extensive treatment. **Dr. Bangalee manages this risk through careful, precise dosing**.

faqs.question: Does medical aid cover hyperhidrosis Botox?
faqs.answer: Some medical aids provide benefit for **clinically diagnosed hyperhidrosis (ICD-10 code R61)**. We recommend contacting your scheme directly and requesting pre-authorisation. We can provide all necessary clinical documentation to support your claim.

faqs.question: Can I use deodorant after the treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes — as usual. Botox does not interact with antiperspirant or deodorant. **Many patients switch to a lighter deodorant** once treatment is effective, as heavy antiperspirant is no longer necessary.

faqs.question: How quickly can I book for excessive sweating treatment?
faqs.answer: Contact us at **+27 (0)31 573 1325** or info@staraesthetic.co.za. We will book a consultation and **typically treat in the same appointment** if you are medically suitable. Allow 2 weeks for full effect before any important event.

faqs.question: Is this the same Botox used for wrinkles?
faqs.answer: Yes — the same **Botulinum Toxin Type A**. The difference is **technique and target**: for hyperhidrosis, small doses are injected intradermally (into the skin) at closely spaced intervals across the sweat zone, rather than deeper into muscles as with cosmetic Botox.

title: Jaw & Chin Contouring
tagline: Define Your Profile — Non-Surgical Jawline & Chin Enhancement in Durban
quickSummary: **Jaw and chin contouring** uses dermal fillers to add definition and projection to an underdeveloped chin or jawline, and Botox to slim an over-developed **masseter muscle** (square jaw) — creating a more balanced, harmonious facial profile.
priceFrom: From R 4,600 per syringe · HarmonyCA R 11,990

notes: Volume and number of syringes required is determined at consultation. Filler longevity is based on product guidelines and may vary between patients.

downtimeDetail: **24–48 hours** mild swelling and possible bruising (cover with makeup from day 2). Avoid strenuous exercise, excessive heat, and pressure on treated areas for 48 hours.
suitableFor: Women wanting a more defined, sculpted **jawline** and feminine facial oval.
Men desiring stronger **jaw definition and chin projection**.
Those whose **chin is receding or weak in profile**, making the nose appear prominent.
Women with a heavy or **square jaw (masseter hypertrophy)** wanting a more tapered, oval face shape.
Anyone wanting **lower face enhancement** without surgical implants.
heroText: A well-defined jawline and balanced chin are among the most requested aesthetic improvements by both women and men. At Star Aesthetic Centre, **Dr. Bangalee** offers a combination of **dermal fillers and Botox** to sculpt, define, and balance the lower face — delivering profile transformation without surgery.
whatIs:
**Jaw and chin contouring** is the art of using injectable treatments to improve the balance, definition, and proportion of the lower face. It encompasses three complementary treatments: **chin augmentation** (adding projection to a receding chin using dermal filler), **jawline definition** (building a stronger, more defined jaw contour using filler along the mandibular line), and **jaw slimming** (reducing masseter muscle bulk in patients with a square or heavy jaw using Botox).

The lower face and profile are among the most powerful determinants of overall facial harmony. A weak chin makes the nose appear larger and the neck less defined. A square, heavy jaw can create a masculine appearance in women. The right combination of filler and Botox can dramatically improve **facial proportions** without any surgery.
howWorks: Facial Analysis & Consultation — Dr. Bangalee conducts a detailed **facial proportions assessment** using the Phi ratio and standardised aesthetic measurements. Goals are discussed and a treatment plan designed.
Photographs — Before photos are taken from **multiple angles** for comparison.
Preparation — Treatment area is cleansed. For filler: **topical anaesthetic or dental block** as required. For Botox: no anaesthetic needed.
Chin Filler — Precisely placed **dermal filler** adds projection, height, or width to the chin depending on the individual assessment.
Jawline Filler — Filler is placed along the **mandibular line** to create definition and sharpness.
Masseter Botox — For jaw slimming, Botox is injected bilaterally into the **masseter muscles**. Full slimming effect develops over 4–6 weeks.
Review — A **2–4 week review** allows fine-tuning of results.
expectedResults: **Immediate:** Filler results are visible from treatment, though mild swelling may temporarily obscure the final result. **24–48 hours:** Swelling resolves; filler result clearly visible. **4–6 weeks:** Masseter Botox slimming effect becomes apparent. **3 months:** Full combined result is visible.

faqs.question: Is non-surgical chin augmentation as effective as a chin implant?
faqs.answer: For mild to moderate **chin projection** needs, dermal filler delivers excellent, natural-looking results without surgery. Chin implants provide permanent augmentation and are more appropriate for larger projection needs. Dr. Bangalee will advise which is appropriate for your goals and anatomy — most patients achieve excellent results with filler.

faqs.question: How long does jawline filler last?
faqs.answer: The jawline and chin are **low-movement areas** — filler placed here tends to last **12–18 months** or longer. Individual results vary based on the product used, your metabolism, and lifestyle factors.

faqs.question: Will jaw slimming Botox change how I eat or speak?
faqs.answer: No — **masseter Botox** does not affect normal eating, speaking, or jaw function. The masseter is the primary chewing muscle, and the doses used for slimming reduce its bulk without affecting its function. Some patients notice mild jaw fatigue when chewing very tough foods in the first few weeks — this resolves as the muscle adapts.

faqs.question: Can jaw and chin contouring make my nose look smaller?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of the most powerful yet least discussed effects of **chin augmentation**. A receding chin makes the nose appear more prominent by comparison. Projecting the chin forward and strengthening the jawline creates a more balanced facial profile that makes the nose appear more proportionate — without any nasal surgery.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between jaw contouring for men and women?
faqs.answer: Yes — the aesthetic goals are different. For women, we typically aim to create a more **tapered, oval lower face** with subtle definition. For men, we aim for stronger, more **angular definition**. The techniques are similar but the injection points, volumes, and product selection differ to maintain gender-appropriate aesthetics.

faqs.question: Can jaw and chin filler be dissolved if I don't like the result?
faqs.answer: Yes — **hyaluronic acid fillers** can be dissolved with hyaluronidase (Hyalase) enzyme at any time. This provides complete peace of mind for patients trying filler for the first time.

faqs.question: What is the difference between jaw fillers and jawline Botox?
faqs.answer: **Jawline filler** adds volume and definition along the jaw bone — creating shape and structure. **Masseter Botox** reduces the bulk of the chewing muscle for a slimmer jaw shape. These address different aspects of jaw aesthetics — Dr. Bangalee often combines both in the same session for comprehensive contouring.

faqs.question: How long does jaw slimming Botox take to work?
faqs.answer: Masseter Botox takes **4–6 weeks** to show slimming results as the muscle gradually weakens and shrinks from reduced use. The full slimming effect can continue to develop for up to 3 months. It lasts **4–6 months** initially, with longer duration after repeated treatments.

faqs.question: Are there risks with jaw and chin fillers?
faqs.answer: When performed by an experienced aesthetic physician like **Dr. Bangalee**, jaw and chin filler is safe. Risks include temporary bruising, swelling, and asymmetry — all correctable. The chin and jaw region contains **arteries** that must be avoided — this is why treatment must only be performed by a medically qualified and anatomically trained injector.

faqs.question: Can I combine jaw contouring with Botox for wrinkles in the same session?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — this is very common. Many patients address both **wrinkle relaxation** (forehead, frown, crow's feet) and **lower face contouring** in a single efficient appointment. Dr. Bangalee creates a comprehensive facial aesthetic plan for patients wanting multi-area treatment.

title: Lip Filler Treatment
tagline: Natural-Looking Lip Enhancement — Volume, Definition & Balance
quickSummary: **Lip fillers** use hyaluronic acid (a naturally occurring sugar molecule) to add volume, improve definition, and restore or enhance the natural shape and proportion of the lips — safely, reversibly, and with results lasting **6–12 months**.

rows.label: Lip Sculpting (volume and filler type dependent)
Juvéderm Volite — improves skin quality for 6 – 9 months

notes: Number of syringes required is determined at consultation. Filler longevity is based on product guidelines and may vary between patients.

downtimeDetail: **24–48 hours** mild-to-moderate lip swelling (can be covered with lip gloss). Bruising is possible but not universal. Avoid intense exercise, heat exposure, and alcohol for 24 hours. Avoid dental procedures for 2 weeks.
suitableFor: Women wanting **natural lip enhancement** — more volume, better definition, or improved symmetry.
Those whose **lips have thinned with age** and who want to restore a more youthful lip appearance.
Women with a naturally **thin upper lip** wanting subtle volume.
Anyone wanting to refine the shape of their **lip border, enhance the cupid's bow**, or address lip asymmetry.
Patients wanting to understand their options before committing — **Dr. Bangalee** is happy to start conservatively.
heroText: Fuller, more defined lips are one of the most requested aesthetic treatments worldwide — and when done with the right technique and the right amount of filler, the result is beautiful, natural, and unmistakably you. At Star Aesthetic, **Dr. Bangalee's philosophy for lip filler is always subtlety first** — enhancing what you have rather than creating something that doesn't belong on your face.
whatIs:
**Lip fillers** use hyaluronic acid (HA) — a sugar molecule naturally produced in the body — to add volume, shape, definition, and hydration to the lips. HA filler is injected in small, precise amounts at specific anatomical points to achieve the patient's desired result — whether that is subtle enhancement, proportional balancing, or more significant volume.

**Hyaluronic acid** is reversible (it can be dissolved with hyaluronidase enzyme at any time), gradually breaks down naturally over **6–12 months**, and integrates very naturally into lip tissue. It also attracts and binds water — keeping the lips hydrated and supple.

**Dr. Bangalee's approach** to lip filler is always proportion-based — assessing the natural relationship between upper and lower lip, the lip-to-face ratio, and the individual's facial features to create a result that enhances rather than dominates.
howWorks: Consultation — Goals are discussed and Dr. Bangalee assesses your **lip anatomy, facial proportions**, and natural lip shape to create a personalised filler plan.
Photographs — Multiple angle photos are taken **before treatment**.
Topical Anaesthetic — **EMLA cream** is applied to the lip area for 20–30 minutes. Most modern lip fillers also contain lidocaine for additional comfort during injection.

Injection — Using a fine needle or blunt **cannula**, hyaluronic acid filler is placed precisely at the anatomical points required for your desired result. Injection is slow and controlled for accuracy.
Massage & Shaping — Dr. Bangalee gently shapes the filler immediately after injection to ensure **even distribution** and the desired contour.
Ice & Recovery — Ice is applied to minimise swelling. You will receive **written aftercare instructions**.
expectedResults: **Immediately:** Lips appear swollen and possibly asymmetrical — this is normal. **24–48 hours:** Swelling begins to settle. **72 hours:** Most swelling resolved; result becoming visible. **2 weeks:** Final result — all swelling completely resolved. **Duration: 6–12 months** (varies by individual metabolism, amount of filler, and whether you move your lips frequently through speaking and eating).

faqs.question: Will lip fillers look natural?
faqs.answer: Natural-looking results depend entirely on the injector's **aesthetic judgment, technique, and the amount of filler used**. Dr. Bangalee's philosophy is always proportion-appropriate results — he will never recommend more filler than your facial anatomy can naturally support. If you are concerned, start with **0.5ml** — this is almost always undetectable to anyone who doesn't know your lips before.

faqs.question: Do lip fillers hurt?
faqs.answer: **Topical anaesthetic cream** is applied for 20–30 minutes before treatment, and modern lip fillers contain **lidocaine** (anaesthetic) in the syringe. Most patients rate lip filler discomfort as 3–4 out of 10 — very tolerable. The sensitivity of lips varies significantly between patients.

faqs.question: How long do lip fillers last?
faqs.answer: Typically **6–12 months**. Lips are a very mobile area — all the movement from speaking, eating, smiling, and kissing metabolises filler faster than less active areas. Smaller amounts (0.5ml) may last 6 months; larger volumes (1–2ml) often last 9–12 months.

faqs.question: Can lip fillers be reversed?
faqs.answer: Yes — **hyaluronic acid filler** can be completely dissolved using hyaluronidase (Hyalase) enzyme. Results reverse within **24–48 hours** of the dissolving treatment. This is one of the major advantages of HA filler over permanent lip augmentation methods.

faqs.question: What is the difference between lip filler and a lip flip?
faqs.answer: **Lip filler** adds volume using hyaluronic acid. A **lip flip** uses a very small amount of Botox injected into the orbicularis oris muscle around the upper lip, causing it to slightly evert (flip outward) — giving the illusion of a fuller upper lip border without actually adding volume. A lip flip lasts **6–8 weeks** and costs significantly less (from R 800) — it is a good option for patients wanting subtle upper lip definition without committing to filler.

faqs.question: Can I get lip fillers if I have thin lips naturally?
faqs.answer: Yes — **thin lips** are one of the most common indications for lip filler. Natural anatomy is always respected — Dr. Bangalee will not create a result that looks disproportionate to your facial structure. For naturally thin lips, we recommend starting with **0.5–1ml** and building gradually over 1–2 sessions.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before my lip filler appointment?
faqs.answer: Avoid **blood-thinning medications and supplements** (aspirin, ibuprofen, fish oil, vitamin E, alcohol) for 48–72 hours before treatment to minimise bruising risk. If you have a history of **cold sores (HSV-1)**, inform Dr. Bangalee — you may need antiviral prophylaxis before lip filler treatment.

faqs.question: I am worried about getting 'duck lips.' How do I avoid this?
faqs.answer: **Duck lips** (disproportionate, overfilled lips) result from too much filler, poor placement, or inappropriate product selection. Dr. Bangalee's anatomy-based approach and conservative philosophy eliminates this risk. The key is working with an experienced aesthetic physician who **prioritises your facial harmony** over simply filling. Show photos of results you like — communication is everything.

faqs.question: Can lip fillers help with vertical lip lines?
faqs.answer: Yes and no. Lip filler adds volume that can push out fine **perioral** (around the mouth) lines from within. For deeper, more established lip lines, a combination of lip filler for volume and small amounts of Botox for the vertical lines delivers the best result. Dr. Bangalee frequently combines these in the same session.

faqs.question: How old do I need to be to get lip fillers in South Africa?
faqs.answer: You must be **18 or older** for cosmetic injectable treatments in South Africa. Dr. Bangalee does not treat patients under 18. We request valid ID for all first-time patients.

title: Medi-Lean Medical Weight Loss Programme
tagline: Medically Supervised Weight Management — Real Results, Sustained Long-Term
quickSummary: The **Medi-Lean Weight Loss Programme** combines FDA-approved prescription medication (including **GLP-1 receptor agonists**), personalised nutritional guidance, and regular physician monitoring to deliver meaningful, sustainable weight loss under full medical supervision.
downtimeDetail: None. The programme integrates fully into your normal life.
suitableFor: Adults with a **BMI ≥ 27** and at least one weight-related health condition (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidaemia).
Adults with a **BMI ≥ 30** regardless of comorbidities.
Those who have tried **diet and exercise consistently** and struggled to maintain meaningful weight loss.
Patients wanting **medically supervised, evidence-based weight management** rather than fad diets.
Those wanting to combine weight loss with **aesthetic body contouring** treatments for comprehensive results.
heroText: Weight management is one of the most complex challenges in medicine — and one of the most personally significant. At Star Aesthetic Centre, the **Medi-Lean programme** provides medically supervised weight loss combining **prescription medication, nutritional guidance**, and regular clinical monitoring by **Dr. Bangalee**. This is not a diet — it is a comprehensive **medical weight management solution**.
whatIs:
The **Medi-Lean Weight Loss Programme** is Star Aesthetic's medical approach to weight management — going far beyond 'eat less, move more' to address the clinical, hormonal, and psychological complexity of obesity and overweight.

The programme utilises the latest generation of **prescription weight loss medications** — including **GLP-1 receptor agonists** (semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide) — which have revolutionised the medical management of obesity by addressing the hormonal dysregulation that drives appetite, satiety, and fat storage. These medications are combined with personalised **nutritional coaching**, body composition monitoring, and regular physician-supervised adjustment.

Medi-Lean is appropriate for patients with a **BMI of 27 or above** with weight-related health concerns, or **BMI of 30 and above**. It is a clinical programme — not a beauty treatment — and Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough medical assessment before prescribing any medication.
howWorks: Initial Medical Assessment — Comprehensive review of **medical history, current medications, BMI, body composition**, and blood tests (HbA1c, lipids, thyroid function, etc.).
Personalised Programme Design — Dr. Bangalee designs your personalised protocol: **medication selection, starting dose, nutritional targets**, and activity recommendations.
Prescription Medication — Where clinically appropriate, **prescription weight management medication** is prescribed (GLP-1 receptor agonist or alternative).
Nutritional Guidance — Detailed **dietary guidance** is provided — not a restrictive diet, but a sustainable caloric and macro approach that works with the medication.
Monthly Monitoring Consultations — **Weight, body measurements, blood pressure**, and medication tolerance are monitored at monthly appointments.
Programme Adjustment — Medication dose is **titrated according to response and tolerability**; nutritional guidance is adjusted as the programme progresses.
expectedResults: **Month 1–2:** Initial weight loss of 1–4kg as medication takes effect and dietary changes are adopted. **Month 3–4:** Increasing momentum — 0.5–1.5kg per week in the effective range. **Month 6:** Many patients achieve **8–15% body weight reduction** at this point. **Month 12:** Clinical trials show **15–20%+ body weight reduction** with GLP-1 medications at optimal doses in compliant patients.

faqs.question: What is a GLP-1 receptor agonist and how does it cause weight loss?
faqs.answer: **GLP-1** (Glucagon-Like Peptide 1) is a hormone naturally produced in the gut after eating — it signals the brain to reduce appetite, slows gastric emptying (keeping you feeling full longer), and improves insulin sensitivity. **GLP-1 receptor agonists** (medications like semaglutide/Ozempic® and tirzepatide/Mounjaro®) mimic this hormone at much higher and sustained concentrations, producing significant and consistent **appetite reduction and metabolic benefits**.

faqs.question: Is Ozempic available in South Africa for weight loss?
faqs.answer: **Semaglutide** (the active ingredient in Ozempic® and Wegovy®) is available in South Africa. Supply has been constrained due to global demand — Dr. Bangalee monitors availability and will advise on the currently available formulation at consultation. Alternative GLP-1 medications (including **liraglutide and tirzepatide**) may be available as alternatives.

faqs.question: How much weight can I expect to lose?
faqs.answer: Clinical trials with **GLP-1 receptor agonists** show average weight loss of **10–20% of body weight** over 12 months for compliant patients. Results vary significantly by individual, starting weight, metabolic profile, dietary adherence, and activity levels. Dr. Bangalee will give you a realistic expectation based on your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the medication safe?
faqs.answer: **GLP-1 receptor agonists** are rigorously studied and approved by multiple regulatory bodies including the **FDA**. They are among the most studied weight management medications in history. Dr. Bangalee conducts a thorough medical assessment to confirm suitability and contraindications before any prescription. Regular monitoring ensures safety throughout the programme.

faqs.question: Will I regain the weight when I stop the medication?
faqs.answer: There is evidence that **weight regain** can occur after stopping GLP-1 medications, as the hormonal drivers of appetite return to baseline. This is why we combine medication with **lifestyle and nutritional education** — to build habits that support weight maintenance independently. Many patients continue at a lower **maintenance dose** long-term.

faqs.question: Can I use the weight loss programme alongside body contouring?
faqs.answer: Yes — and this is often the **optimal approach**. Significant weight loss followed by **body contouring** for residual fat deposits and skin tightening delivers comprehensive results. Dr. Bangalee can advise the best sequencing of medical weight loss and aesthetic body treatments.

faqs.question: Do I need blood tests before starting the programme?
faqs.answer: Yes — a **baseline blood panel** is required before initiating prescription medication. This includes **HbA1c** (blood sugar), lipid panel, thyroid function, kidney function, and a full blood count. This ensures safety and also provides a baseline for measuring metabolic improvements as weight loss progresses.

faqs.question: Can the Medi-Lean programme help with Type 2 diabetes?
faqs.answer: **GLP-1 receptor agonists** were originally developed for **Type 2 diabetes** management — weight loss is an additional significant benefit. If you have Type 2 diabetes, these medications may simultaneously improve your **blood glucose control** and support weight loss. This requires careful coordination with your treating physician or endocrinologist.

faqs.question: What dietary guidance is included in the programme?
faqs.answer: Dr. Bangalee provides a **personalised nutritional framework** — not a restrictive crash diet, but a structured approach to **caloric intake, macronutrient balance, and meal timing** that works synergistically with the medication. The focus is on sustainable eating patterns, not short-term food restriction.

faqs.question: Is this programme appropriate for post-menopausal weight gain?
faqs.answer: Yes. **Menopausal weight gain** is driven by hormonal changes that alter fat distribution and metabolic rate — and it is one of the most frustrating and common clinical presentations in an aesthetic medical practice. **GLP-1 medications** work independently of oestrogen levels and are effective for **post-menopausal weight management**.

title: Pigmentation Treatment
tagline: Restore Your Natural Glow — Expert Pigmentation Treatment in Durban
quickSummary: **Pigmentation** — from dark spots to stubborn melasma — is a medically complex problem that needs more than a brightening cream. **Dr. Bangalee** combines the world-renowned **cosmelan® depigmentation system**, professional chemical peels, and a rigorously supervised home-care programme to deliver real, lasting results.

rows.label: Mesoesthetic Melanostop Tranex — Face and eye
Mesoesthetic Melanostop Tranex — Face, eye + Microneedling
Dermaceutic Crystal Peel — Face and neck
Dermaceutic Mela Forte Peel — Face and neck

notes: Numbing cream available: R 110. Treatment modality is selected at consultation based on pigmentation type and severity.

downtime: Cosmelan: 5–7 days peeling/sensitivity; Peels: 2–5 days; Dermapen: 24–48 hours
downtimeDetail: **cosmelan® mask:** 5–7 days of peeling, redness, and sensitivity. **Chemical peels:** 2–5 days of mild peeling and sensitivity. **Dermapen:** 24–48 hours redness. Home-care products: occasional mild sensitivity during adaptation phase.
suitableFor: Women with **melasma** — hormonally-driven pigmentation triggered or worsened by the pill, pregnancy, or perimenopause.
Those with **post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation** from acne, trauma, or previous treatments.
Anyone with **sun-induced dark spots and uneven skin tone** — particularly relevant in KZN's high-UV coastal environment.
**All Fitzpatrick skin types** — our protocols are specifically adapted for South African skin diversity, including darker tones.
Those who have spent money on **over-the-counter brightening products** that delivered little to no results and are ready for medical-grade intervention.
heroText: You've spent **years and hundreds of rands** on brightening creams that promise everything. You've watched the dark patches hold their ground while everything else in your life moved forward. You've heard 'just use sunscreen' so many times it's almost insulting. **Pigmentation — especially melasma — is not a simple problem.** It is a chronic, medically complex condition that requires a structured medical programme to address properly. In **Durban's relentlessly high-UV coastal environment**, managing pigmentation without the right clinical tools is a losing battle. At Star Aesthetic Centre, **Dr. Bangalee treats pigmentation the way it deserves** — systematically, with the right tools, and with honest expectations about what it takes to get there.
whatIs:
There is something deeply personal about **carrying pigmentation on your face**. It's the first thing you see in every mirror, every photograph, every reflective shop window. For many women it quietly erodes the confidence you carry into interviews, promotions, client meetings, and important photographs. **How you feel about your skin affects how you present yourself** — and the research supports what many women already know.

Medically, hyperpigmentation is the result of **excess melanin production** in localised areas of the skin. It is caused by UV exposure (solar lentigines, or sun spots), hormonal fluctuations (**melasma — the 'mask of pregnancy'**), post-inflammatory triggers (dark marks after acne), or a combination of all three. In South Africa — with its **high UV index year-round** and a diverse population spanning Fitzpatrick skin types III–VI (all of which are prone to melanin excess) — pigmentation is the most common aesthetic concern in clinical practice.

**Treating pigmentation without daily SPF is like bailing out a boat with the tap still running.** UV exposure rebuilds dark spots as fast as treatment removes them. This is why our approach is always a programme — combining professional in-clinic depigmentation with a non-negotiable home-care protocol anchored by **melan tran3x and mesoprotech® melan 130 SPF50+**.

Our in-clinic treatments include the globally recognised **cosmelan® depigmentation system**, professional chemical peels, and Dermapen microneedling with brightening serums — all selected and adapted for South African skin diversity.
howWorks: Comprehensive Pigmentation Assessment — Dr. Bangalee uses **clinical examination and Wood's lamp where appropriate** to identify the type and depth of your pigmentation, assess Fitzpatrick skin type, and design a personalised programme.

Home-Care Programme Initiation — **melan tran3x Concentrate and mesoprotech® melan 130 SPF** are prescribed immediately to begin depigmentation and establish daily SPF compliance before in-clinic treatment begins — non-negotiable.

In-Clinic Treatment — Depending on pigmentation type and severity: **cosmelan® intensive depigmentation mask** (single in-clinic application), professional chemical peels (mesoestetic mesopeel), or Dermapen with brightening serums.
Post-Treatment Support — Full aftercare protocol provided. **Daily SPF compliance** is the cornerstone of every pigmentation programme without exception.
Progress Monitoring — **Photograph comparison at each visit** tracks measurable improvement — results become visible and motivating over the programme.
Maintenance Programme — Ongoing **skin-tone maintenance** through continued home-care and periodic in-clinic treatment — because managing pigmentation is a long game, and one worth playing.
expectedResults: **Week 1–2:** Home-care depigmenting actives begin working; cosmelan® peeling phase if applicable. **Week 3–4:** Early fading of surface pigmentation; skin tone becoming visibly more even. **Week 6–8:** **Significant reduction in dark spot intensity**; improved overall radiance. **Week 10–12:** Substantial pigmentation correction with continued improvement through ongoing home-care. Note: **melasma** is a chronic condition — ongoing management prevents recurrence.

faqs.question: What is the best treatment for melasma in Durban?
faqs.answer: The most effective approach is a **combination programme**: cosmelan® (or professional peels) for intensive in-clinic depigmentation, **melan tran3x Concentrate** for daily home-care, and **mesoprotech® melan 130 SPF50+** for year-round UV protection. **Treating any one element in isolation produces partial results.** Book a consultation with Dr. Bangalee for your personalised programme.

faqs.question: Why does my pigmentation keep coming back?
faqs.answer: Pigmentation — especially **melasma** — is a **chronic condition, not a one-time problem**. The melanocytes (pigment-producing cells) that were overactive remain primed to re-respond to UV and hormonal triggers. Without consistent **daily SPF** and an ongoing home-care protocol, pigmentation recurs. This is not treatment failure — **it is the nature of the condition**, and why maintenance matters.

faqs.question: Is it safe to treat pigmentation on darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — **with the right protocols**. Aggressive lasers or high-strength peels can cause paradoxical darkening in **Fitzpatrick IV–VI skin**. At Star Aesthetic, we use protocols specifically appropriate for South African skin diversity — including the **melan tran3x system and mesoestetic mesopeel** — formulated and clinically tested for darker skin types.

faqs.question: What is cosmelan® and how is it different from a regular peel?
faqs.answer: **cosmelan®** is mesoestetic's globally recognised **intensive depigmentation system** — the most widely used professional depigmentation treatment in the world. Unlike chemical peels that primarily exfoliate, cosmelan® **acts directly on the melanin production pathway** through multiple mechanisms simultaneously, delivering significantly more targeted depigmentation. Applied in-clinic as a single treatment, followed by a specific home-care maintenance protocol.

faqs.question: Do I really need to wear sunscreen every day?
faqs.answer: Yes — **non-negotiably**. UV exposure is the primary driver of pigmentation. Without **daily SPF50+**, every in-clinic treatment and home-care product is partially undone by daily sun exposure. **In Durban's UV environment, daily sun protection is not optional** — it is the single most powerful depigmenting tool available.

faqs.question: How long does pigmentation treatment take?
faqs.answer: Meaningful results require **a minimum of 8–12 weeks** of consistent treatment. Full results typically develop over **3–6 months** of a complete programme. Pigmentation treatment is a commitment — but the results of a properly followed programme are **transformative and lasting** when maintained.

faqs.question: Can the pill or HRT cause pigmentation?
faqs.answer: Yes. **Hormonal contraceptives and HRT** are known triggers for melasma in susceptible women. The combination of exogenous oestrogen and UV exposure activates melanocytes in hormonally sensitive individuals. Changing contraceptive method may be part of the management approach — discuss this with both **Dr. Bangalee** and your gynaecologist.

faqs.question: Can I treat pigmentation during pregnancy?
faqs.answer: Pregnancy itself is a common cause of **melasma** ('mask of pregnancy'). Treatment options during pregnancy are **restricted** — many brightening actives (retinoids, AHAs, hydroquinone) are contraindicated. Safe options are limited to gentle skincare and strict **SPF use**. Dr. Bangalee will advise the safest approach for pregnant patients.

faqs.question: Will skin peels help with my dark spots?
faqs.answer: Yes — **professional chemical peels** (salicylic, glycolic, mandelic, or mesoestetic mesopeel) accelerate cell turnover and remove pigmented surface cells. Multiple peel sessions combined with home-care produce meaningful lightening of superficial dark spots. **Deeper pigmentation (melasma reaching the dermis)** requires more intensive treatment such as cosmelan®.

faqs.question: Is there a permanent solution to pigmentation?
faqs.answer: Superficial sun spots can be **substantially cleared** with the right treatment programme. **Melasma**, however, is a chronic condition — the tendency to produce excess melanin in response to UV and hormonal triggers is lifelong. With the right ongoing programme (**SPF + home-care + periodic in-clinic maintenance**), melasma can be very effectively suppressed — but it requires consistent, committed management.
