heroText: There is a moment — usually in a certain light, or a certain photo — when **your skin just doesn't look like you feel**. You feel capable, energetic, ready for whatever is next. But the skin looking back at you carries a heaviness that doesn't match. For many women, that gap between how they feel inside and how they look outside has a **quiet but real impact on confidence** — in the boardroom, on a first impression, in client-facing moments that matter. **A professional chemical peel is one of the simplest ways to close that gap.** At Star Aesthetic Centre, Dr. Bangalee uses the **mesoestetic mesopeel® range** — the gold standard in professional peels — to deliver precisely calibrated results for your specific skin and your specific life.
whatIs:
A chemical peel is a **controlled application of a carefully selected acidic solution** that exfoliates the skin's outer layers, removes accumulated dead and damaged cells, breaks up pigment deposits, and **triggers the skin's renewal response** — stimulating new cell growth and collagen production that brings fresher, brighter skin to the surface.

The depth of the peel — superficial, medium, or deep — is **precisely calibrated** to your skin concern, type, and tolerance. At Star Aesthetic, we use the **mesoestetic mesopeel® professional range** — the globally recognised benchmark in professional chemical peels, developed by the original formulators of the glycolic acid peel. The mesopeel® range includes specific formulations for **pigmentation, acne, anti-ageing, and sensitive skin** — engineered for your specific concern, not a generic one-size-fits-all solution.

The result is skin that doesn't just look better — it **feels different**. Lighter. More alive. More like the person you already are on the inside.
howWorks: Consultation & Skin Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your skin type, **Fitzpatrick classification, and specific concerns** to select the most appropriate peel formulation.
Pre-Peel Preparation — For deeper peels, 2–4 weeks of **home-care priming** (retinol, AHA) may be recommended to improve results and reduce complications.
Skin Cleansing & Degreasing — The skin is thoroughly cleansed with a **pre-peel solution** to ensure even penetration and consistent results.
Peel Application — The selected peel solution is applied in **controlled layers**. You will feel warmth, tingling, or mild stinging — all expected and a sign the peel is active.
Neutralisation — Some formulations require neutralisation with a bicarbonate solution; others **self-neutralise** at the correct depth.
Post-Peel Treatment — **Soothing serum, moisturiser, and SPF50+** are applied. Written aftercare instructions are provided before you leave.
expectedResults: **Day 1:** Skin appears flushed — a mild 'sunburn' sensation is normal. **Days 2–4:** Peeling begins — this is new skin emerging, not damage. **Days 5–7:** Peeling complete; **brighter, fresher skin visible**. **Week 2 onwards:** Continued improvement as collagen remodelling progresses. **Peak results** at 2–4 weeks post-peel, with cumulative improvement after each session in a course of **3–6 peels**.

faqs.question: What does a chemical peel actually do to my skin?
faqs.answer: A chemical peel applies an acidic solution that **breaks the bonds between old, dead skin cells**, causing controlled exfoliation. The removal of outer skin layers triggers the skin's natural renewal response — stimulating cell turnover, **new collagen production**, and the emergence of fresh, undamaged skin from beneath. The result is brighter, smoother, more even-toned skin.

faqs.question: Is a chemical peel safe for darker skin tones?
faqs.answer: Yes — with the right peel selection. Higher-strength **TCA peels** carry risk of **post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) in darker skin types**. At Star Aesthetic, we use the **mesoestetic mesopeel® range** which includes specific formulations optimised for Fitzpatrick III–VI skin — including **mandelic acid peels**, preferred for darker skin due to their lower PIH risk.

faqs.question: How long does the peeling last and what does it look like?
faqs.answer: Peeling typically begins **2–4 days after treatment** and lasts 3–7 days depending on peel depth. Skin sheds in patches — it may look like dry, flaking skin rather than dramatic sheets. This is the natural process of **old, damaged cells shedding to reveal fresh skin**. Do not forcibly pick — let it shed naturally.

faqs.question: Can I wear makeup after a chemical peel?
faqs.answer: Avoid all makeup for the **first 24 hours**, and only mineral makeup for days 2–5. During the active peeling phase, allow skin to breathe. Once peeling is complete (typically **day 5–7**), normal makeup use can resume.

faqs.question: What can I not do after a chemical peel?
faqs.answer: For 7–14 days post-peel: **no direct sun exposure**, no swimming pools (chlorine), no saunas, no strenuous exercise. Avoid active skincare ingredients (retinol, AHAs, BHAs, Vitamin C) unless directed otherwise. **SPF50+ is mandatory every morning** — non-negotiable.

faqs.question: Which is better — a chemical peel or Dermapen microneedling?
faqs.answer: **Different tools for different goals.** Chemical peels excel at improving surface texture, pigmentation, and skin tone through exfoliation and cell renewal. Dermapen excels at stimulating deeper collagen production for wrinkle reduction, scar treatment, and skin density improvement. Many patients benefit from **alternating both treatments** throughout the year for comprehensive skin rejuvenation.

faqs.question: Will a chemical peel help my acne?
faqs.answer: Yes — **salicylic acid peels** are particularly effective for acne-prone skin, penetrating into pores to dissolve sebum and keratin plugs. **Glycolic and mandelic acid peels** also improve acne through accelerated cell turnover. Multiple sessions are needed for lasting improvement.

faqs.question: How many peels do I need?
faqs.answer: A course of **3–6 peels spaced 2–4 weeks apart** is recommended for meaningful improvement in most skin concerns. A single peel provides a refreshing result but sustained improvement requires a series. Dr. Bangalee will recommend the appropriate number for your specific concern.

faqs.question: Is there any peel I can have done and still go to work the next day?
faqs.answer: Yes — **superficial glycolic or mandelic acid peels** cause only mild redness and minimal to no peeling. Most patients return to work the next day looking 'refreshed' rather than having visible peel signs. These are sometimes called **'lunch-hour peels'** and are ideal for busy professionals.

faqs.question: Can peels help with open pores?
faqs.answer: Yes — regular chemical peels, particularly **salicylic and glycolic acid peels**, reduce enlarged pores by clearing congestion and stimulating the collagen that supports the pore walls. Combined with a **retinol home-care programme** (skinretin 0.3%), pore refinement over 3–6 months is significant and lasting.

title: Varicose Veins Treatment
tagline: Non-Surgical Thread Vein & Varicose Vein Management in Durban
quickSummary: **Sclerotherapy** — the injection of a sclerosing agent directly into unwanted veins — is the gold standard non-surgical treatment for **spider veins (telangiectasias)** and small-to-medium varicose veins, causing them to collapse and fade over **4–12 weeks**.
downtime: Minimal — compression stockings worn for 5–7 days
downtimeDetail: **Compression stockings** for 5–7 days (worn continuously for the first 48 hours, then during the day). Avoid swimming, hot baths, saunas, and sunbathing for 2 weeks post-treatment. Walking is encouraged — avoid prolonged standing or sitting.
suitableFor: Adults with cosmetically bothersome **spider veins or thread veins** on the legs.
Those with **small to medium varicose veins** causing cosmetic distress or mild symptoms.
Patients experiencing **leg aching, heaviness, or night cramps** associated with visible vein disease.
Those who have been assessed and are **not surgical candidates** or prefer a non-surgical approach.
**Women post-pregnancy** where varicose veins have developed or worsened.
heroText: Varicose and spider veins affect approximately **1 in 3 adults** and can cause both cosmetic distress and physical symptoms — aching, heaviness, swelling, and cramping in the legs. At Star Aesthetic Centre, **Dr. Bangalee** offers non-surgical assessment and treatment options for **thread veins and smaller varicose veins**.
whatIs:
**Varicose veins** are enlarged, twisted veins that result from failed valves in the venous system — blood pools in the vein rather than flowing efficiently back to the heart, causing the vein to dilate and become visible through the skin. **Spider veins (telangiectasias)** are smaller, superficial vessels visible as red, purple, or blue patterns.

**Sclerotherapy** is the injection of a sclerosing (irritating) solution directly into the unwanted vein. The solution damages the inner lining of the vessel, causing it to collapse, stick together, and gradually be absorbed by the body over **4–12 weeks** — leaving the skin clear where the vein once was.

**Dr. Bangalee** performs a thorough vascular assessment before treatment to ensure that the veins being treated are appropriate for sclerotherapy and that there is no significant underlying **venous insufficiency** that requires referral to a vascular surgeon.
howWorks: Vascular Assessment — Dr. Bangalee assesses your veins clinically (and with **Duplex ultrasound referral** if indicated) to determine the extent of venous disease and suitability for sclerotherapy.
Treatment Mapping — The veins to be treated are identified and marked with the **patient standing**.
Injection — Using a very fine needle, a **sclerosing solution** is injected directly into each targeted vein. Multiple injections are typically performed per session.
Compression Application — Following treatment, **graduated compression stockings** are applied immediately and worn continuously for 5–7 days to optimise outcome.
Follow-Up — Results are assessed at **4–6 weeks**, and further sessions are planned as needed.
expectedResults: **Week 1–2:** Treated veins appear inflamed and possibly darker — this is the inflammatory response and is expected. **Weeks 3–6:** Veins begin to fade as they are absorbed. **Weeks 6–12:** Progressive fading continues; treated veins largely or completely invisible. **Multiple sessions** required for extensive veins.

faqs.question: Does sclerotherapy hurt?
faqs.answer: Each injection causes a brief sting and a 2–3 second cramp-like sensation as the solution enters the vein. Most patients find this very tolerable. The procedure takes **30–60 minutes** and most patients return to normal activities immediately.

faqs.question: How many sessions will I need?
faqs.answer: This depends entirely on the extent and number of veins to be treated. **Spider veins** may require 1–3 sessions. More extensive **varicose veins** typically require 3–5 sessions. Dr. Bangalee will give you a clear estimate at your assessment.

faqs.question: Can varicose veins come back after treatment?
faqs.answer: Treated veins **do not return** — once a vein is successfully sclerosed it is eliminated. However, varicose vein disease is progressive — new veins can develop over time, particularly with standing occupations, pregnancy, or genetic predisposition. Periodic maintenance sessions manage new vein development.

faqs.question: When can I see results?
faqs.answer: Treated veins will temporarily appear worse (darker, more prominent) for **1–2 weeks** as the inflammatory response occurs. Progressive fading begins at week 3–4 and continues to weeks 8–12. Final results for each session are assessed at **6–8 weeks**.

faqs.question: Do I need to wear compression stockings and why?
faqs.answer: Yes — **compression stockings** are mandatory post-sclerotherapy. They press the treated vein walls together, improving the collapse and absorption of the sclerosed vein, reducing bruising and side effects, and preventing **deep vein thrombosis**. They must be worn continuously for the first 48 hours and during waking hours for 5–7 days.

faqs.question: Can I exercise after sclerotherapy?
faqs.answer: **Walking** is actively encouraged immediately post-treatment — it activates the calf muscle pump and improves venous return. Avoid strenuous exercise (running, cycling, gym training) for **48 hours**. Swimming and hot baths are avoided for 2 weeks.

faqs.question: Are there risks with sclerotherapy?
faqs.answer: Sclerotherapy has an excellent safety record when performed by a trained physician. Possible side effects include temporary bruising, **brown pigmentation** along the treated vein (hyperpigmentation, usually resolves in 3–12 months), small ulcers at injection sites (rare), and **telangiectatic matting** (formation of small new vessels — uncommon). Serious complications (DVT, allergic reaction) are rare with modern sclerosing agents.

faqs.question: Can varicose veins be treated without surgery?
faqs.answer: Yes — **sclerotherapy** is effective for spider veins and small to medium varicose veins without any surgery. Large, symptomatic varicose veins with significant underlying venous reflux may require surgical or **endovenous laser treatment (EVLT)**, which Dr. Bangalee may refer to an appropriate vascular surgeon if indicated.

faqs.question: Does medical aid cover varicose vein treatment?
faqs.answer: **Symptomatic varicose veins** (pain, swelling, heaviness) may qualify for medical aid benefit under specific ICD-10 codes. Purely cosmetic spider vein treatment is typically not covered. Dr. Bangalee will provide the appropriate clinical documentation for medical aid submission where indicated.

faqs.question: I am pregnant — can I treat my varicose veins now?
faqs.answer: No. **Sclerotherapy is contraindicated** during pregnancy and breastfeeding. Additionally, varicose veins often improve significantly in the **3–6 months after delivery** as pregnancy-related venous pressure resolves. We recommend waiting until 3 months post-delivery and post-breastfeeding before commencing treatment.
